song.title: Wrecked Outright
song.lyrics:
[V1]1. 1) “Wrecked outright On Jesus’ breast”; Only “wrecked” souls thus can sing; Little boats that hug the shore, Fearing what the storm may bring, Never find on Jesus’ breast, All that “wrecked” souls mean by rest.
[V2] 2) “Wrecked outright!” So we lament; But when storms have done their worst, Then the soul, surviving all, In eternal arms is nursed; There to find that nought can move One, embosomed in such love.
[V3] 3) “Wrecked outright!” No more to own E’en a craft to sail the sea; Still a voyager, yet now Anchored to Infinity; Nothing left to do but fling Care aside, and simply cling.
[V4] 4) “Wrecked outright!” ’Twas purest gain, Henceforth other craft can see That the storm may be a boon, That, however rough the sea, God Himself doth watchful stand, For the “wreck” is in His hand.
song.author: Margaret E. Barber
